name: Soba Noodle Salad
instructions:
1. Whisk together the rice vinegar, vegetable oil, sesame oil, brown sugar, soy sauce, and grated fresh ginger in a large bowl until fully combined and the sugar dissolves, creating a flavorful dressing. Set aside.

2. Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Add the soba noodles and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender but still slightly chewy, 4 to 5 minutes. Drain through a colander and immediately rinse under cold running water to stop the cooking and remove excess starch.

3. Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced chicken breast strips and cook, stirring frequently, until the chicken is no longer pink and cooked through, about 2 to 4 minutes. Add the minced garlic, salt, and black pepper, and continue stirring for 1 more minute until the garlic becomes fragrant.

4. In a large serving bowl, combine the cooled soba noodles, cooked chicken, sliced celery, shredded carrot, diced red bell pepper, chopped fresh cilantro, sliced green onion, and sesame seeds. Pour the dressing over the top and gently toss to evenly coat all ingredients. Serve immediately or chill for more developed flavors.
simpleInstructions:
1. Whisk vinegar, oils, sugar, soy sauce, and ginger until smooth for dressing.

2. Boil soba noodles until tender, drain, and rinse with cold water.

3. Stir-fry chicken strips until cooked, then add garlic and seasonings.

4. Toss noodles, chicken, fresh veggies, and dressing together, then serve chilled or at room temp!
